Refactor the `eventbus` package to thread a dispatch `Context` through the entire handler call chain. Concretely, make ALL of these changes consistently across the package:

1. Rename the core handler method `Handler.handle(self, event)` to `Handler.process(self, event, context)` in `eventbus/handler.py`, and update `safe_handle` to `safe_process(self, event, context)` which calls `self.process(event, context)`. The old `handle` / `safe_handle` names must be gone.

2. Update every concrete handler in `eventbus/handlers/` (audit.py, email.py, metrics.py, webhook.py) to define `process(self, event, context)` instead of `handle(self, event)`. Each handler MUST actually use the context: include the context's `correlation_id` in what it records/returns (AuditHandler: add `"cid": context.correlation_id` to its trail entry dict; EmailHandler: append `" cid:<correlation_id>"` to the message string; MetricsHandler: also record the last seen correlation id on an attribute `self.last_cid = context.correlation_id`; WebhookHandler: add `"cid": context.correlation_id` to each queued item dict).

3. Change the middleware API in `eventbus/middleware.py`: `dispatch(self, handler, event, next_call)` becomes `dispatch(self, handler, event, context, next_call)` for the base `Middleware` and both `LoggingMiddleware` and `CountingMiddleware`, forwarding `context` to `next_call(handler, event, context)`. `LoggingMiddleware` must record `(handler.name(), event.topic, context.correlation_id)` triples.

4. Update `eventbus/dispatcher.py` so `run` and the terminal call and the `_wrap` closure all accept and forward `context`: the terminal must call `handler.safe_process(event, context)`, and each middleware is called with the context. Use `context.child()` when passing the context deeper into each middleware layer so that `context.depth` reflects how many middlewares wrapped the call (outermost middleware sees the original depth, the terminal/handler sees depth == number of middlewares).

5. Update `eventbus/bus.py` so `publish` passes the per-publish `Context` it already builds into `dispatcher.run(handler, event, context)`.

Keep `eventbus/util.py` and `eventbus/version.py` exactly as they are. After the refactor, `EventBus.publish` must still return the list of per-handler results and behave the same, except the context is now genuinely threaded through and used.
